bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a top-utility bid that protects my most important issueA and the other high-value issues. The opponent’s initial offer uses valueF on every issue, which is very low utility for me, and their cooperative message is not backed by any concession pattern yet, so I will make a firm opening counter.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior all-valueA offer. The opponent has held firm on all valueF, which is very low utility for me, and their messages are cooperative but not backed by any numerical concession, so I will not make a major move yet.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.04 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain clustered at very low-value outcomes for me, and their latest change is not a meaningful concession from my perspective, so I treat the partnership language as cheap talk rather than evidence of flexibility. I avoid repeating my last bid by conceding slightly on issueC while holding firm on my highest-priority issueA and other strong values.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low-value for me, with no meaningful concession despite cooperative/emotional messaging. I am making a small additional concession on issueC, where several non-top values still retain decent utility, while holding firm on my highest-priority issueA and other strong preferences.

I am targeting a still-strong early utility around 0.76, since time is only 0.08 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held firm on low-value-for-me bids, but their latest move on issueC to valueC suggests some willingness to move there; I will match that signal and add a modest concession on lower-priority issueB while holding firm on issueA and issueD. Their messages are cooperative but cheap talk, so I am relying mainly on the actual offer pattern.

I am still targeting a relatively high utility because it is very early (t=0.10), but I am making a modest concession from my strongest bids to test whether the opponent will move. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and only show small, inconsistent movement, so I am holding firm on my most valuable issueA and issueD while conceding somewhat on issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.76 because it is early in the negotiation (t=0.12), so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has only made limited concessions, mainly on issueC and issueD, while holding issueA and issueB near my worst values; their emotional messages are cheap talk, so I am relying on the bid pattern instead. I keep my most important issueA and valuable issueD at valueA, while offering issueC at valueC, which they have already proposed, and a modest movement on issueB.

I am targeting a still-positive utility around 0.63 by holding firm on my highest-weight issueA while conceding further on issueD and keeping moderate concessions on issueB and issueC. Time is still early at t=0.14, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s very low-utility offers, but their repeated bids suggest they strongly prefer lower values on issueB/issueD and have only made small concessions so far. Their messages are cooperative but cheap talk, so I am relying mainly on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the mid-0.6 range because it is early in the negotiation at t=0.16, so there is no need to concede near the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent has made only small concessions and their offers remain poor for me, though they seem to push hard for low values on issueB and issueD; I will continue conceding mainly on lower-cost dimensions while holding issueA at my top value.

I am targeting a weighted utility around 0.61, which is a continued but moderate concession this early at t=0.18 rather than moving near the opponent’s very low offers. The opponent’s bids have improved only slightly for me and still cluster around low values, while their messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I am trusting the offer pattern more than the language. I am holding firm on my most important issueA while conceding further on issueB and keeping issueD at a value they have repeatedly proposed.

I am targeting a utility around 0.56, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier bids but still protects my highest-weight issueA. At t=0.20 there is still time, so I should not accept the opponent’s low-utility framing, but their offers consistently indicate they strongly want issueB=valueF and issueD=valueF, while they have shown willingness to move issueC to valueC. Their messages are cooperative but cheap talk, so I am relying mainly on the bid pattern and offering concessions on lower-priority issues while holding issueA firm.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.55, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA, my most important issue. At t=0.22 there is still time, but the opponent’s offers consistently signal they want low values on issueA/issueD and have recently moved to issueC:valueC and issueB:valueE, so I use those signals without accepting their emotional appeals at face value.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.53 while making a visible concession on issues the opponent has repeatedly emphasized through their offers, especially issueB=valueF, issueC=valueC, and issueD=valueE/F. Since it is still early at t=0.24, I should not concede my highest-weight issueA away from valueA yet; the opponent’s messages are emotional appeals, but their bid history shows only gradual movement and a persistent demand for low-utility values on B/D.

I am targeting a utility a little above 0.5, since we are still only at t=0.26 and I should not give up my highest-weight issueA yet. The opponent’s offers show repeated pressure toward low values on issueA/issueB and some movement to issueC:valueC and issueD:valueE, so I am matching their recent signals on issueB, issueC, and issueD while holding firm on issueA:valueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.48, which is a meaningful concession from my best bids but still well above reservation this early-mid stage. The opponent’s offers have improved only modestly for me and their messages are mostly emotional/partnership framing, but their bidding pattern suggests they strongly want concessions on issueA and prefer low-ranked values for issueB/issueD, so I am testing a controlled move on issueA while keeping issueC at a decent value.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.51, which is a meaningful concession from my ideal while still protecting the high-weight issueA at t=0.30. The opponent’s offers have improved slightly for me, especially on issueA and issueC, but they continue to push hard for issueB/valueF and issueD/valueF, so I concede those lower-priority areas while countering with issueA/valueB. Their messages emphasize partnership and urgency, but I am relying on the bid history rather than the emotional framing.

I am targeting a moderate concession around 0.49 utility for me: still protecting issueA with valueB because it is my highest-weight issue, while conceding more on issueB and issueD where the opponent has repeatedly signaled interest in lower-ranked values for me. At t=0.32 there is some time pressure but not enough to collapse to the opponent’s low-utility offers; their messages are cooperative but the actual offers remain far below my interests, so I am making a tradeoff rather than accepting their framing.

At t=0.34 we are still in the mid-game, so I am targeting a moderate utility rather than dropping to the opponent’s low offers. The opponent has shown some movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD, but their natural-language appeals are cheap talk; their actual bids suggest they still prefer lower-ranked values for me, so I will hold issueA at valueB while using values they have recently signaled on the other issues.

I am targeting a utility just above 0.50, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier bids but still protects my highest-weight issueA by keeping valueB. At t=0.36 there is time left, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s latest low-utility offer; however, their bids show persistent preference for issueB=valueF and issueD=valueF, so I concede those while holding issueC at a decent compromise value.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around the mid-game rather than accepting the opponent’s still-low latest offer. Their bids have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueA and issueC, but their messages are mainly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer pattern instead. At t=0.38 I can concede on issueB, issueC, and issueD while still protecting the most important issueA with valueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64: still favorable for me at mid-game, but with meaningful concessions on issueA, issueC, and issueD. The opponent’s latest offer surprisingly included issueB: valueA, which is my best value there, so I am treating that as a signal it may be acceptable while holding concessions on the issues where they have repeatedly pushed toward valueF/valueC. Their messages are cooperative but not binding, so I rely mainly on the offer pattern.

At t=0.42 we are still in the mid-game, so I am targeting a reasonably high-utility bid while incorporating concessions the opponent has actually signaled. The opponent’s latest offer moved issueC to valueB and previously offered issueB:valueA, so I combine those with my continued preference for a strong issueA position and a concession on issueD, while discounting the emotional partnership language as non-binding.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.57, conceding materially from my best outcomes while still protecting issueA, my most important issue. The opponent’s offers have consistently favored low-value choices for me on issueD and often issueB, while recently showing willingness on issueC=valueB, so I am combining their signaled preferences on issueB/issueD with a strong but not maximal stance on issueA. Their messages are cooperative but repetitive cheap talk, so I rely mainly on the offer pattern rather than the emotional framing.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65 while still making a credible concession on the lower-priority issueD, where the opponent repeatedly insists on valueF. The opponent’s messages are mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offers: they have shown willingness to give issueB:valueA and some movement on issueC, but remain difficult on issueA and issueD; I am holding issueA at valueB because it is my highest-weight issue.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.52: below my earlier demands but still safely above reservation value, appropriate for the mid-game at t=0.48. The opponent’s latest valid offer moved substantially toward me on issueA and issueC, while their history suggests they often prefer issueD: valueF, so I keep their apparent issueB preference and switch issueD back to valueF, which is also better for me than valueE. Their messages are cooperative/emotional but not binding, so I am relying on the actual bid pattern.
